title: Citrus Roasted Mexican Chestnut Burrito
description: A citrusy, fluffy, postworkout Mexican dish featuring roasted chestnuts and a burst of fresh mango, wrapped in a warm flour tortilla.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss chestnuts with olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, and chili powder; spread on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, turning halfway, until golden and shells blister. Remove, let cool, then peel and chop into bite‑sized pieces.
In a skillet over medium heat, warm the cooked brown rice and black beans, seasoning with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Warm each tortilla in a dry skillet or microwave for 15 seconds until pliable.
In a large bowl, combine the roasted chestnuts, rice & beans, shredded lettuce, diced mango, lime juice, and cilantro; toss gently to mix.
Place about 1/4 cup of the mixture onto the center of each tortilla, fold in the sides and roll tightly.
If desired, sear each burrito seam‑side down in a lightly oiled skillet for 2 minutes per side until golden.
Slice each burrito diagonally and serve warm.
